summary: Backend refactoring Phase 3 verification complete. Admin Members, Household, and Reviews modules extracted from server.py. Fixed duplicate route conflict in product_routes.py. 35/35 tests passed.

bugs_fixed_by_testing_agent.issue: Duplicate POST /api/reviews endpoint conflicting with review_routes.py - returned 'review_id' instead of 'review' object
bugs_fixed_by_testing_agent.fix: Removed duplicate endpoint from product_routes.py, kept the complete implementation in review_routes.py which includes admin notifications and user association

endpoints_tested: GET /api/admin/members - Returns list of all customers with stats ✓
POST /api/admin/members/{user_id}/gift - Gifts membership to a member ✓
GET /api/household/{user_email} - Returns household info with pets ✓
POST /api/household/{user_email}/add-pet - Adds new pet to household ✓
GET /api/household/{user_email}/recommendations - Returns safe products for all pets ✓
POST /api/reviews - Creates new review (anonymous or authenticated) ✓
GET /api/reviews/my-reviews - Returns user's reviews (requires JWT auth) ✓
GET /api/products/{product_id}/reviews - Returns approved reviews for product ✓
GET /api/admin/reviews - Returns all reviews for admin ✓

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: Duplicate POST /api/reviews endpoint in product_routes.py was conflicting with review_routes.py
All extracted routes properly exclude MongoDB _id from responses using {'_id': 0} projection
Database injection pattern (set_database function) is consistent across all extracted files
Admin authentication properly implemented using HTTPBasic with secrets.compare_digest
JWT authentication works correctly with Authorization: Bearer header in review_routes.py
Admin member routes support both user_id and email for member lookup (flexible querying)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend refactoring Phase 3 complete. Admin Members, Household, and Reviews modules extracted from server.py. Test file created at /app/backend/tests/test_admin_household_review_routes.py for regression testing. All endpoints verified working with proper JWT and Basic Auth. Fixed duplicate route conflict in product_routes.py.
